latest.title: Granting of non-exclusive licence to HUI
latest.content:
13 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0570A
Powerhouse Energy Group PLC
13 April 2026
13 April 2026
Powerhouse Energy Group plc
("Powerhouse" or the "Company")
Granting of non-exclusive licence to Hydrogen Utopia International PLC
Powerhouse Energy Group plc (AIM: PHE),
a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy with a revenue generating engineering consulting division ("Engsolve")
,
confirms that after reviewing the potential opportunities with Hydrogen Utopia International PLC ("HUI"), the Company has granted a non-exclusive licence to HUI. This licence will enable HUI to promote the Company's Distributed Modular Generation ("DMG") technology across selected European markets.
The granting of the non-exclusive licence to HUI not only allows the current exciting opportunities to be investigated, but also the development of additional opportunities in central Europe.
For clarity, this is a non-exclusive licence agreement enabling HUI to market the DMG technology across selected European markets. The operator/developer of each project utilising the DMG technology would still need to pay the additional operating licence fees.
Further announcements will be made in due course as appropriate.
Paul Emmitt, CEO of Powerhouse, Commented:
"This was an unexpected opportunity which was presented to us but given the requirement for identifying non-fossil fuel energy supplies, is excellent timing. This provides PHE, at no cost, access to a team of experienced people who are knowledgeable of our technology in central Europe at a time when the EU market is looking for ways to become self-sufficient on energy products.
We look forward to working with them to deliver on the considerable opportunities available in the region."
-ENDS-
For more information, contact
:
Powerhouse Energy Group Plc
Paul Emmitt, CEO

About Powerhouse Energy Group Plc
Powerhouse Energy has developed a process technology which can utilise waste plastic, end-of-life-tyres, and other waste streams to convert them efficiently and economically into syngas from which valuable products such as chemical precursors, hydrogen, electricity, heat and other industrial products may be derived. PHE's process produces low levels of safe residues and requires a small operating footprint, making it suitable for deployment at enterprise and community level.
Powerhouse also incorporates Engsolve Ltd, which is a revenue generating business offering Engineering Services across all sectors, with speciality services in the development of new technologies and clean energy.
For more information see
www.phegroup.com

latest.title: MENA, GCC and Asia Marketing Agreement
latest.content:
2 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8138U
Powerhouse Energy Group PLC
02 March 2026
2nd March 2026
Powerhouse Energy Group plc
("Powerhouse" or the "Company")
MENA, GCC and Asia Marketing Agreement
Abraham Accord linked company to market PHE in MENA, GCC and Asia
Powerhouse Energy Group plc (AIM: PHE),?a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy with its revenue generating engineering consulting subsidiary ("Engsolve"), is pleased to announce that it has signed a marketing agreement with Green Gecko Energy Limited ("Green Gecko"), a UK based consultancy, which will act as the Company's sole agent in introducing Powerhouse to potential opportunities across the Abraham Accord countries, the wider MENA and GCC region, and certain countries within Asia.
Highlights:
·
Green Gecko was formed by David Scott and Peter Nutt and in the past 12 months they, and Sir Liam Fox, who is an adviser to the company, have been working in the Abraham Accords signatory countries, the wider MENA and GCC region and certain countries within Asia to identify a number of projects to exclusively utilise the Powerhouse high-temperature pyrolysis (HTP) modular solution.
·
Projects currently identified for Powerhouse by the Green Gecko team are located in Bahrain, Egypt, Kuwait and Oman; and address market niches including:
o
pilot sites, predominately to address the processing of tyres and other elastomers and/or polymers as well as plastic(s); and
o
deployment of high-temperature pyrolysis (HTP) modular solutions, which can be expanded as performance and offtake markets are proven.
·
No up-front funding is required from the Company.
·
Agreement is on a "no win, no fee" basis, where any payment by the Company to Green Gecko will be made on a commission basis, and satisfied through a combination of cash and options over up to 3% of the Company's current share capital (exercisable at a price of 0.5 pence) wherein specific construction related milestones (and associated payments to PHE) are achieved by the projects introduced by Green Gecko.
Commenting on the Agreement, Paul Emmitt, Powerhouse CEO, said:
"We are delighted to enter into this agreement with the Green Gecko team. MENA is a huge market, and our technology has the potential to provide a solution to the green energy needs of this region. David and Peter are highly connected and experienced in the area and we believe that with them we will gain significant traction for our business model and offering.
"We look forward to working with the Green Gecko team in further spreading our geographic footprint as this relationship delivers the opportunities that we believe are inherent in the region for our technology."
Peter Nutt and David Scott, Directors of Green Gecko Energy said:
"We both are delighted to enter into this agreement with Powerhouse Energy.? We have a number of projects which we anticipate bringing to the Company from our relationships in the Abraham Accord countries, MENA, GCC region and Asia.?
"We look forward to working with Paul and the Team in the year ahead."
About Green Gecko Energy
Green Gecko Energy is a private limited company, incorporated last year, founded by David Scott and Peter Nutt with a strategy to market Powerhouse's technology and model in the Abraham Accord Countries, MENA and the wider GCC region and Asia.
David Scott has over 40 years of experience in corporate broking and investment banking industry, advising small and medium-sized companies listed on the London Stock Exchange and AIM Market. He has extensive expertise in private equity, mergers and acquisitions, and strategic advisory work for investors and management teams in the UK and internationally.
He currently serves as a Partner at Chayton Capital LLP, an FCA-regulated investment firm, and CEO of the Abraham Accords Prosperity Group Limited, which is currently developing three businesses, (i) Prosperity Microgrid Partners, which will develop the Green Gecko opportunity, (ii) Prosperity Reach Partners, which is focused on delivering service sector solutions, initially in Bahrain and Morocco and (iii) a fund, focused on creating employment opportunities for young people and businesses in the GCC Region.
Peter Nutt has many decades experience in corporate finance advising and arranging experience having worked from bases in the City of London, Sydney, Australia and twice from Tokyo, Japan. He is very familiar with complex cross-border financing structures having arranged many lease and debt transactions, particularly in the aviation sectors international flag carriers. This knowledge coupled with senior contacts around the world will be used for the benefit of Green Gecko.
-ENDS-
For more information, please visit
www.phegroup.com

PHE's process produces low levels of safe residues and requires a small operating footprint, making it suitable for deployment at enterprise and community level.
Powerhouse also incorporates Engsolve Ltd, which is a revenue generating business offering Engineering Services across all sectors, with speciality services in the development of new technologies and clean energy.

latest.content:

RNS Number : 6261Q
Powerhouse Energy Group PLC
28 January 2026
28 January 2026
Powerhouse Energy Group plc
("Powerhouse" or the "Company")
Submission of Planning Application
Powerhouse Energy Group plc (AIM: PHE),
a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy with a revenue generating engineering consulting division ("Engsolve")
,
is pleased to announce that it has today submitted a full planning application to the Mid and East Antrim Borough Council for the proposed 40 tonnes per day (TPD) waste-to-hydrogen facility at Silverwood Business Park, Ballymena, Northern Ireland.
Extensive design and engineering work has already been completed to enable the submission and below are some renderings of the proposed facility which are included in the planning application.
The next stage, on receiving responses from the first round of planning consultations, will be the preparation of the Permit application to the Northern Ireland Environment Agency (NIEA) and the Company will make a further announcement at that time.

latest.title: Signing of Agreement to Lease Site in Ballymena
latest.content:
26 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2379Q
Powerhouse Energy Group PLC
26 January 2026
26 January 2026
Powerhouse Energy Group plc
("Powerhouse" or the "Company")
Signing of Agreement to Lease Site in Ballymena
Powerhouse Energy Group plc (AIM: PHE),
a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy with a revenue generating engineering consulting division ("Engsolve")
,
is pleased to announce that it has signed an agreement to lease (the "Lease Agreement") for a 1.98 acre serviced site at Silverwood Business Park, Ballymena, Northern Ireland.
Terms of the Lease Agreement:
·
Site owner: Silverwood Business Park Ltd.
·
Site address: Silverwood Business Park, Ballymena, County Antrim.
·
Annual rent:
An initial rent-free period of up to 12 months and, thereafter, subject to five yearly reviews.
·
Length of agreement:
Lease is for a 25-year term.
·
Break clause:
A tenant break clause on the fifth anniversary of term commencement.
Powerhouse plans to build, own and operate a 40 tonnes per day (TPD) waste to hydrogen facility at the site. The facility is intended take 40 TPD of waste destined for landfill and convert it into 99.999% pure hydrogen for a variety of uses, including transport. Discussions are already taking place on feedstock and offtake agreements.
The Company will shortly submit the required planning application to the Mid and East Antrim Borough Council, and a further announcement will be made once this has been submitted. Approval of the planning application is a condition to the Lease Agreement becoming effective.
Commenting on the Lease Agreement, Paul Emmitt, Powerhouse CEO, said:
"This is a major step forward for the Company. Ballymena is set to become the Hydrogen Hub of Northern Ireland, with the organisations including Wrightbus, Translink and the Mid and East Antrim Borough Council all focused on developing the hydrogen economy in Northern Ireland."
-ENDS-
For more information, please visit
www.phegroup.com
or

company_30s.what_they_do: PHE Ltd. develops and commercialises advanced hydrogen production technology, mainly through its DMG (Direct Methane to Hydrogen) platform, which turns natural gas into clean hydrogen and syngas. They also offer related engineering services for plant design and operation.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Hydrogen is a key pillar of the UK’s net‑zero strategy and the global shift to low‑carbon fuels. PHE’s technology can turn abundant methane resources into green hydrogen, giving it a competitive edge in the growing hydrogen market and in regions looking to decarbonise their energy mix.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early commercialisation phase, building sites and securing licences. It is expanding its footprint through international agreements and is actively seeking partners to scale up production. The shares are trading very low, reflecting the company’s pre‑revenue or early‑revenue status and the high uncertainty around its commercial viability.

rns_narrative: PHE is moving from a technology‑development focus to a partnership‑driven growth strategy. The latest announcements show a non‑exclusive licence with Hydrogen Utopia International (HUI) and a marketing agreement covering the MENA, GCC and Asian regions, signalling a push to secure early commercial contracts.

Operationally, the firm is finalising key sites: it has leased a plant in Ballymena, completed a FEED for a new ammonia‑hydrogen project, and updated its Australian project pipeline. These steps indicate a tangible path toward first‑of‑its‑kind commercial deployment.

Governance and financial reporting are keeping pace: the company has released audited results, updated its board composition, and announced a new advisory team, suggesting a stabilising corporate structure that can support the upcoming growth phase.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is quietly bullish but largely silent, waiting for the first commercial milestone.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight the fresh licensing deal with HUI and the expanding regional agreements as evidence that the technology is gaining traction and could unlock a large, under‑served market.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the company’s lack of revenue, the long lead times for plant construction, and the uncertainty around whether the DMG process can be scaled cost‑effectively.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from speculative chatter to focused, data‑driven optimism. After the HUI licence announcement, posts have become more confident, but the overall volume remains low, indicating that the market is still on the sidelines.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker are flat and dead, meaning there is no current public buzz. This silence creates a high‑surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The first commercial hydrogen output from the Ballymena lease, if achieved within the next 12 months, would validate the DMG platform and likely trigger a price rally.
Any announcement that the MENA or Asian marketing agreements have moved into binding contracts or that a pilot plant has begun production would be a strong catalyst.
Positive audit or earnings updates that show a clear path to profitability or a significant capital raise would also boost confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months are: (1) a spike in trading volume accompanying a news release, (2) the company’s press releases confirming a signed commercial contract or a production milestone, and (3) any regulatory approvals or environmental clearances for the planned sites.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the very early phase of a deep‑crash recovery cycle. A strong catalyst such as a first‑of‑its‑kind commercial output could trigger a sharp bounce. The APEX score suggests a tight price compression, so a breakout above the current low could be a clear confirmation of mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company hits a RED‑type crash bottom, historical data shows that such crashes tend to return roughly 160% on average. A mid‑point recovery to the level between the current low and the previous high would deliver that upside. The recent licensing and operational milestones reduce the probability of a prolonged stagnation, increasing the odds of a rapid rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main downside is that the technology is still unproven at scale, and construction delays or cost overruns could push the company into a cash‑flow crunch. If the company fails to secure a commercial contract or if regulatory hurdles stall the Ballymena site, the price could stay flat or fall further.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price, while a successful bounce to the midpoint would yield more than double the investment. The asymmetry is roughly 2:1 in favour of the upside, assuming the company completes its first commercial output.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the current low after a volume spike, size the position to risk no more than 2–3% of your portfolio per trade, and place a stop just below the recent swing low. Use a trailing stop that locks in gains once the price moves 30% above entry.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until a clear commercial milestone is announced – e.g., a signed contract, a production start‑up, or a positive audit result. Watch for a volume surge of at least 150% above the 20‑day average accompanying the news.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks have been identified, so no pass‑through condition is set. The deep crash and lack of current downside risk keep the setup bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: PHE is a deep‑crashed, low‑sentiment play that could rebound sharply once it demonstrates a commercial hydrogen output, offering a high‑asymmetry upside if the company’s partnership and operational milestones materialise.
